title: Campanula tenerrima
taxonomy: Campanula tenerrima is classified within the family Campanulaceae, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family consists of over 2,300 species, making it one of the largest plant families. Campanula tenerrima is further classified within the genus Campanula, which consists of approximately 500 species. The genus Campanula is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in temperate regions and are known for their bell-shaped flowers. Campanula tenerrima is a member of this diverse group, adding to the rich variety of plant species within the Campanulaceae family.

intro: Campanula tenerrima, also known as Baby's Tears, is a small, delicate flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its calming and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Campanula tenerrima include flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Baby's Tears is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic properties.
